title: lib.rs feature table claims linux-dev enables /dev/tpm0 but Device::open is always available
description: wolftpm/src/lib.rs documents 'linux-dev | Linux /dev/tpm0 kernel driver transport'. But Device::open() in device.rs has no #[cfg(feature = 'linux-dev')] guard — it is always compiled. The linux-dev feature only controls whether wolftpm-sys/wolftpm-src compile wolfTPM with WOLFTPM_LINUX_DEV defined; at runtime, if the feature is not set and wolfTPM autodetects, Device::open() still works. The feature table description implies 'without linux-dev, /dev/tpm0 is unavailable', which is false. This will confuse users who read the docs. The table entry should say 'linux-dev | compile wolfTPM with explicit Linux kernel driver transport (default: autodetect)'.
